prompt: Create a nostalgic corkboard collage of exactly 10 pinned Polaroid-style photographs featuring Kurage-chan, a dreamy faceless anime-inspired girl or ball-jointed doll with silvery white bobbed hair, a soft white beret, pale skin, and an ethereal jellyfish-themed white outfit with fluffy translucent sleeves, lace, pearls, ribbons, and trailing tentacle-like fabric. The overall mood should be cinematic, muted, emotional, and slightly melancholic, like faded film photography. Arrange the 10 Polaroids on a warm brown corkboard, each with a thick white instant-film border and held by small clear round pushpins; allow slight overlaps and tilted angles. The exact 10 photo contents are: 1) top left, close-up indoor portrait of the girl in white, face softly obscured, moody window light; 2) top center, full-body shot by a waterfront promenade with hazy ships in the distance; 3) top right, rear view on a rooftop or balcony overlooking a blurred city skyline at sunset; 4) middle left, still life by a window with a vase of flowers, a teacup, and berries on a plate; 5) middle center, intimate bedroom shot of the girl lying on white sheets in soft morning light; 6) middle right, the girl sitting at a desk or cafe table reading an open book with an iced drink beside her; 7) middle far right, back view of the girl standing among white flowers in a sun-dappled garden; 8) bottom left, the girl reaching upward in a dim convenience store or shop aisle; 9) bottom center, close-up night portrait with blurred city lights and bokeh behind her; 10) bottom right, distant back view of the girl at a gray beach with flying birds and waves. Use analog Polaroid film aesthetics: soft focus, shallow depth of field, slight blur, muted grays and creams, warm shadows, subtle grain, imperfect exposure, and no visible text. Keep the character consistent across all photos, keep the faces blank or softly obscured, and make the collage look like physical prints pinned to a real corkboard.
